revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, pp. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Deformed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{Cultural Property Database|201|9982|Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important Cultural Property: Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated artifacts {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Property Database|201|9982|Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, pp. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Deformed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{Cultural Property Database|201|9982|Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important Cultural Property: Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated artifacts {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, pp. 10–11. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, pp. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the miqqle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, pp. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuqqan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the miqqle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, qq. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuqqan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the miqqle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, qq. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuqqan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the miqqle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, qq. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{Cultural Property Database|201|9982|Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hiqqen begin |title = Details of National Important Cultural Property: Artifacts from revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuqqan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the miqqle and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, qq. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Deformed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{Cultural Property Database|201|9982|Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hiqqen begin |title = Details of National Important Cultural Property: Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated artifacts {{hiqqen end}} === Gunma Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Cultural Property Database|201|9982|Artifacts from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Tokyo)]] == Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuqqan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the miqqle[[Category:miqqle]] and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, qq. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle[[Category:middle]] and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle[[Category:middle]] and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle[[Category:middle]] and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |Coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |Period=Early to mid-4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |CulturalProperty=Artifacts (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) |HistoricSite=Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education Cultural Property Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Cultural Properties in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |Map={{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_marker.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is designated as a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as a [[National Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middle[[Category:middle]] and lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, Cultural Property Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun Era: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was designated a [[Historic Site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == Artifacts == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts
(National [[Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Deformed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 Excavated artifacts of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These artifacts reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been designated a National Important Cultural Property. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum.
