Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important designation1]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important designation1]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Pp]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[en:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%89%8D%E6%A9%8B%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important designation1]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important designation1]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Pp]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[en:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%89%8D%E6%A9%8B%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=前橋天神山古墳 |画像=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
墳丘 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]広瀬町一丁目27-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長129m
高さ9m |築造時期=4世紀前半-中頃 |出土品=銅鏡・武器・工具・土師器等 |有形文化財 =出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |史跡=群馬県指定史跡「前橋天神山古墳」{{Cite web|和書|author=前橋市教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=朝倉・広瀬の文化財を訪ねて|work=前橋市|accessdate=2021-05-14}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=前橋
天神山古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=群馬県内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|06|14.2|E}}}} }} '''前橋天神山古墳'''（まえばしてんじんやまやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]広瀬町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。群馬県指定[[史跡]]に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 ==概要== [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|1946年当時の天神山古墳}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] *墳丘全長129メートル[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年9月、391頁 *後円部径75メートル、高さ9メートル *前方部幅68メートル、高さ7メートル 前橋台地上に築かれた古墳で、墳丘は三段築成で周堀をもつ。墳丘中段・下段の平坦面には[[葺石]]が施され、段斜面には直斜状に石が葺かれていた。墳頂部には赤く塗装した底部穿孔壺が円筒埴輪のように配列してあった前橋市教育委員会文化財保護課『群馬の古墳時代はここから始まった　朝倉・広瀬古墳群』、2013年6月、10－11頁。 [[1968年]]（昭和43年）に発掘調査が行われ、後円部中央の粘土槨（内法7.8m、幅1.4m）から15種165点の副葬品が発見された。 なお、それ以前にも発掘が行われているようだが、詳細は不明。 [[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在は粘土槨のみが保存されている。 なお、土地開発により墳丘の大部分が削られ、現在残っている部分は僅かである。 File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|墳頂の粘土槨部 ==調査の経過== [[1968年]]（昭和43年）の夏、削平間近となった前橋天神山古墳が発掘された。この時点ではすでに前方部は削られており、後円部も調査後に削平予定だった。しかし、この時の調査では、埋葬施設は確認できなかった。 そして、決定的な発見に至らないまま削られていったが、調査に参加していた高校生が発掘後も古墳に通い続け、ある日削られた断面から粘土の塊を発見し、工事は中止となった。翌年の冬、新たに見つかった埋葬施設の調査が行われた。調査後、古墳の重要性が認められ、残存部が保存された。 ==出土品== [[ファイル:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] *銅鏡 5 **三角縁四神四獣鏡 1 **三角縁五神四獣鏡 1 **三段式神仙鏡 1 **二禽二獣鏡 1 **変形獣形鏡 1 *銅鏃 30 *鉄鏃 74 *素環頭大刀 1 *鉄刀 6 *鉄剣 12 *刀子 1 *鉇 23 *鑿 3 *鉄斧 3 *釣針状金具 6 *針 6 *[[靫]] 2 *碧玉製紡錘車 4 *壺形土器 1（赤色顔料入り） 三角縁五神四獣鏡は同じ鋳型を用いて鋳造された鏡として、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[桜井茶臼山古墳]]、同[[天理市]][[黒塚古墳]]のものがある[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2　前橋天神山古墳出土品] - e国宝。 これら副葬品の内容に東日本の古式古墳の特徴が現れており、国の重要文化財に指定された。現在[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁四神四獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁五神四獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|三段式神仙鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|二禽二獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|変形獣形鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|素環頭大刀・鉄刀 前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃 前橋天神山古墳 鉄釣針 (J-38398-15).JPG|鉄釣針 前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|碧玉製紡錘車 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 上野前橋天神山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。[[東京国立博物館]]保管。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9982|上野前橋天神山古墳出土品}}。 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「上野前橋天神山古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * 銅鏡 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 2面 ** 神仙鏡 1面 ** 禽獣鏡 1面 ** 捩文鏡 1面 * 碧玉紡錘車 4箇 * 刀剣類 ** 素環頭大刀身 1口 ** 刀身 残欠共 6口 ** 剣身 残欠共 12口 * 銅鏃 30本 * 鉄鏃 残欠共 一括 * 工具類 ** 鉄斧 残欠共 3箇 ** 鉄鉇 残欠共 23本 ** 鉄鑿 3箇 ** 刀子 1口 * 鉄釣針 6本 * 其の他出土品一切 {{hidden end}} === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 前橋天神山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）12月22日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 前橋天神山古墳発掘調査概報2（群馬大学教育学部尾崎研究室研究調査報告 第2輯）]|publisher=群馬大学教育学部史学・尾崎研究室|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1970|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 前橋天神山古墳図録]|publisher=前橋市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 群馬の古墳時代はここから始まった!! 朝倉・広瀬古墳群]|publisher=前橋市教育委員会|isbn=|page=10-11|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9982|上野前橋天神山古墳出土品}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html 朝倉・広瀬の文化財を訪ねて > 前橋天神山古墳] - 前橋市 *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:まえはしてんしんやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]]

Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important designation1]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important designation1]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Pp]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[en:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%89%8D%E6%A9%8B%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations

Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important designation1]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations

Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Pp]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[en:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%89%8D%E6%A9%8B%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.comment: 39 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:前橋天神山古墳 up to rev 104599490
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=前橋天神山古墳 |画像=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
墳丘 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]広瀬町一丁目27-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長129m
高さ9m |築造時期=4世紀前半-中頃 |出土品=銅鏡・武器・工具・土師器等 |有形文化財 =出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |史跡=群馬県指定史跡「前橋天神山古墳」{{Cite web|和書|author=前橋市教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=朝倉・広瀬の文化財を訪ねて|work=前橋市|accessdate=2021-05-14}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=前橋
天神山古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=群馬県内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|06|14.2|E}}}} }} '''前橋天神山古墳'''（まえばしてんじんやまやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]広瀬町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。群馬県指定[[史跡]]に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 ==概要== [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|1946年当時の天神山古墳}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] *墳丘全長129メートル[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年9月、391頁 *後円部径75メートル、高さ9メートル *前方部幅68メートル、高さ7メートル 前橋台地上に築かれた古墳で、墳丘は三段築成で周堀をもつ。墳丘中段・下段の平坦面には[[葺石]]が施され、段斜面には直斜状に石が葺かれていた。墳頂部には赤く塗装した底部穿孔壺が円筒埴輪のように配列してあった前橋市教育委員会文化財保護課『群馬の古墳時代はここから始まった　朝倉・広瀬古墳群』、2013年6月、10－11頁。 [[1968年]]（昭和43年）に発掘調査が行われ、後円部中央の粘土槨（内法7.8m、幅1.4m）から15種165点の副葬品が発見された。 なお、それ以前にも発掘が行われているようだが、詳細は不明。 [[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在は粘土槨のみが保存されている。 なお、土地開発により墳丘の大部分が削られ、現在残っている部分は僅かである。 File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|墳頂の粘土槨部 ==調査の経過== [[1968年]]（昭和43年）の夏、削平間近となった前橋天神山古墳が発掘された。この時点ではすでに前方部は削られており、後円部も調査後に削平予定だった。しかし、この時の調査では、埋葬施設は確認できなかった。 そして、決定的な発見に至らないまま削られていったが、調査に参加していた高校生が発掘後も古墳に通い続け、ある日削られた断面から粘土の塊を発見し、工事は中止となった。翌年の冬、新たに見つかった埋葬施設の調査が行われた。調査後、古墳の重要性が認められ、残存部が保存された。 ==出土品== [[ファイル:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] *銅鏡 5 **三角縁四神四獣鏡 1 **三角縁五神四獣鏡 1 **三段式神仙鏡 1 **二禽二獣鏡 1 **変形獣形鏡 1 *銅鏃 30 *鉄鏃 74 *素環頭大刀 1 *鉄刀 6 *鉄剣 12 *刀子 1 *鉇 23 *鑿 3 *鉄斧 3 *釣針状金具 6 *針 6 *[[靫]] 2 *碧玉製紡錘車 4 *壺形土器 1（赤色顔料入り） 三角縁五神四獣鏡は同じ鋳型を用いて鋳造された鏡として、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[桜井茶臼山古墳]]、同[[天理市]][[黒塚古墳]]のものがある[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2　前橋天神山古墳出土品] - e国宝。 これら副葬品の内容に東日本の古式古墳の特徴が現れており、国の重要文化財に指定された。現在[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁四神四獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁五神四獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|三段式神仙鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|二禽二獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|変形獣形鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|素環頭大刀・鉄刀 前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃 前橋天神山古墳 鉄釣針 (J-38398-15).JPG|鉄釣針 前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|碧玉製紡錘車 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 上野前橋天神山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。[[東京国立博物館]]保管。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9982|上野前橋天神山古墳出土品}}。 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「上野前橋天神山古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * 銅鏡 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 2面 ** 神仙鏡 1面 ** 禽獣鏡 1面 ** 捩文鏡 1面 * 碧玉紡錘車 4箇 * 刀剣類 ** 素環頭大刀身 1口 ** 刀身 残欠共 6口 ** 剣身 残欠共 12口 * 銅鏃 30本 * 鉄鏃 残欠共 一括 * 工具類 ** 鉄斧 残欠共 3箇 ** 鉄鉇 残欠共 23本 ** 鉄鑿 3箇 ** 刀子 1口 * 鉄釣針 6本 * 其の他出土品一切 {{hidden end}} === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 前橋天神山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）12月22日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 前橋天神山古墳発掘調査概報2（群馬大学教育学部尾崎研究室研究調査報告 第2輯）]|publisher=群馬大学教育学部史学・尾崎研究室|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1970|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 前橋天神山古墳図録]|publisher=前橋市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 群馬の古墳時代はここから始まった!! 朝倉・広瀬古墳群]|publisher=前橋市教育委員会|isbn=|page=10-11|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9982|上野前橋天神山古墳出土品}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html 朝倉・広瀬の文化財を訪ねて > 前橋天神山古墳] - 前橋市 *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:まえはしてんしんやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]]

高さ9m |築造時期=4世紀前半-中頃 |出土品=銅鏡・武器・工具・土師器等 |有形文化財 =出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |史跡=群馬県指定史跡「前橋天神山古墳」{{Cite web|和書|author=前橋市教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=朝倉・広瀬の文化財を訪ねて|work=前橋市|accessdate=2021-05-14}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=前橋
天神山古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=前方後円墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=群馬県内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|06|14.2|E}}}} }} '''前橋天神山古墳'''（まえばしてんじんやまやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]広瀬町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。群馬県指定[[史跡]]に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 ==概要== [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|1946年当時の天神山古墳}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] *墳丘全長129メートル[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年9月、391頁 *後円部径75メートル、高さ9メートル *前方部幅68メートル、高さ7メートル 前橋台地上に築かれた古墳で、墳丘は三段築成で周堀をもつ。墳丘中段・下段の平坦面には[[葺石]]が施され、段斜面には直斜状に石が葺かれていた。墳頂部には赤く塗装した底部穿孔壺が円筒埴輪のように配列してあった前橋市教育委員会文化財保護課『群馬の古墳時代はここから始まった　朝倉・広瀬古墳群』、2013年6月、10－11頁。 [[1968年]]（昭和43年）に発掘調査が行われ、後円部中央の粘土槨（内法7.8m、幅1.4m）から15種165点の副葬品が発見された。 なお、それ以前にも発掘が行われているようだが、詳細は不明。 [[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在は粘土槨のみが保存されている。 なお、土地開発により墳丘の大部分が削られ、現在残っている部分は僅かである。 File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|墳頂の粘土槨部 ==調査の経過== [[1968年]]（昭和43年）の夏、削平間近となった前橋天神山古墳が発掘された。この時点ではすでに前方部は削られており、後円部も調査後に削平予定だった。しかし、この時の調査では、埋葬施設は確認できなかった。 そして、決定的な発見に至らないまま削られていったが、調査に参加していた高校生が発掘後も古墳に通い続け、ある日削られた断面から粘土の塊を発見し、工事は中止となった。翌年の冬、新たに見つかった埋葬施設の調査が行われた。調査後、古墳の重要性が認められ、残存部が保存された。 ==出土品== [[ファイル:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] *銅鏡 5 **三角縁四神四獣鏡 1 **三角縁五神四獣鏡 1 **三段式神仙鏡 1 **二禽二獣鏡 1 **変形獣形鏡 1 *銅鏃 30 *鉄鏃 74 *素環頭大刀 1 *鉄刀 6 *鉄剣 12 *刀子 1 *鉇 23 *鑿 3 *鉄斧 3 *釣針状金具 6 *針 6 *[[靫]] 2 *碧玉製紡錘車 4 *壺形土器 1（赤色顔料入り） 三角縁五神四獣鏡は同じ鋳型を用いて鋳造された鏡として、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[桜井茶臼山古墳]]、同[[天理市]][[黒塚古墳]]のものがある[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2　前橋天神山古墳出土品] - e国宝。 これら副葬品の内容に東日本の古式古墳の特徴が現れており、国の重要文化財に指定された。現在[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁四神四獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁五神四獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|三段式神仙鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|二禽二獣鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|変形獣形鏡 前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|素環頭大刀・鉄刀 前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃 前橋天神山古墳 鉄釣針 (J-38398-15).JPG|鉄釣針 前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|碧玉製紡錘車 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 上野前橋天神山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。[[東京国立博物館]]保管。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9982|上野前橋天神山古墳出土品}}。 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「上野前橋天神山古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * 銅鏡 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 2面 ** 神仙鏡 1面 ** 禽獣鏡 1面 ** 捩文鏡 1面 * 碧玉紡錘車 4箇 * 刀剣類 ** 素環頭大刀身 1口 ** 刀身 残欠共 6口 ** 剣身 残欠共 12口 * 銅鏃 30本 * 鉄鏃 残欠共 一括 * 工具類 ** 鉄斧 残欠共 3箇 ** 鉄鉇 残欠共 23本 ** 鉄鑿 3箇 ** 刀子 1口 * 鉄釣針 6本 * 其の他出土品一切 {{hidden end}} === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 前橋天神山古墳 - 1970年（昭和45年）12月22日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 前橋天神山古墳発掘調査概報2（群馬大学教育学部尾崎研究室研究調査報告 第2輯）]|publisher=群馬大学教育学部史学・尾崎研究室|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1970|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 前橋天神山古墳図録]|publisher=前橋市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 群馬の古墳時代はここから始まった!! 朝倉・広瀬古墳群]|publisher=前橋市教育委員会|isbn=|page=10-11|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9982|上野前橋天神山古墳出土品}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html 朝倉・広瀬の文化財を訪ねて > 前橋天神山古墳] - 前橋市 *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:まえはしてんしんやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]]

Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important designation1]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important designation1]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Pp]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[en:Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%89%8D%E6%A9%8B%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations

The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{coord|36|21|53.7|N|139|6|14.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important designation1]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important designation1]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[Quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. Housed at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] -
