[[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17221367]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{Coord|36.364917|N|139.103944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17221367}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17221367]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{Coord|36.364917|N|139.103944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17221367}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Mound |location=[[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{Coord|36.364917|N|139.103944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17221367}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17221367}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location=[[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{Coord|36.364917|N|139.103944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site]] by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. Housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] in Sakurai City and [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]] in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17221367}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17221367]] Wikidata name: Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location={{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{Coord|36.364917|N|139.103944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site}} by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site}} by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁四神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三角縁五神四獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts File:前橋天神山古墳出土 三段式神仙鏡.JPG|Three-tiered mythical mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 二禽二獣鏡.JPG|Two birds and two beasts mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 変形獣形鏡.JPG|Detypeed animal-shaped mirror File:前橋天神山古墳出土 素環頭大刀・鉄刀.JPG|Sword with simple ring pommel and iron swords File:前橋天神山古墳出土 鉄剣・鉄鏃・銅鏃.JPG|Iron blades, iron arrowheads, and bronze arrowheads File:前橋天神山古墳出土 碧玉紡錘車.JPG|Jade spindle whorls == Historic site == === National Important designation1 === * burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Details below. Housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun - Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - Maebashi City *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Japan (Tokyo)]] == See Also == * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前橋天神山古墳|version=104599490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17221367}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. Designated December 22, 1970.{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/000389307.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Historic site in Gunma]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63218 Preliminary Report on the Excavation of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Gunma University, Department of History|year=1968|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16093 Catalogue of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=1970|ref=}} * {{Cite book|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16308 The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups]|publisher=Maebashi City Board of Education|year=2015|pages=10-11|ref=}} == External Links == * {{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/kurashi/230/266/269/p003163.html Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose > Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - [[co:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Maebashi Tenjin-yama Kofun]][[ja:前橋天神山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun.JPG|250px]]
Mound |location={{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Hirose Town, 1-Chome 27-7 |coordinates={{Coord|36.364917|N|139.103944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 129 m
Height: 9 m |built=Early to mid-4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, weapons, tools, earthenware, etc. |Historic Site=burial goods (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |Historic Site=Gunma Prefecture Historic site "Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun"{{Cite web|author=Maebashi City Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/5/11384.html|title=Exploring Historic site in Asakura and Hirose|publisher=Maebashi City|access-date=2021-05-14}} |map={{location map|Japan Gunma|map_dot_label=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=21|coordinates=53.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=06|lon_sec=14.2|mark=Keyhole_kofun_omitted.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=location within Gunma Prefecture}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun''' (前橋天神山古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Hirose Town, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture. It is Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site}} by Gunma Prefecture, and its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a [[National Important designation1]]. == Overview == [[File:461028 M Tenjinyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Tenjinyama Kofun in 1946}}{{small|{{Aerial Photograph of National Territory}}.}}]] * Total mound length: 129 metersOtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun). Tokyo: Tokyodo Shuddan, 1989, p. 391. * Rear circular section: Diameter 75 meters, Height 9 meters * Front rectangular section: Width 68 meters, Height 7 meters The kofun was constructed on the Maebashi Plateau, featuring a three-tiered mound surrounded by a moat. The flat sections of the middleand lower tiers were covered with fukiishi (paving stones), with the slopes paved in a straight diagonal arrangement. The top of the mound had jars with red painted interiors, arranged in rows similar to cylindrical haniwa.Maebashi City Board of Education, designation1 Division. ''Gunma no Kofun Jidai wa Koko Kara Hajimatta Asakura Hirose Kofungun'' (The Beginning of Gunma's Kofun built: Asakura and Hirose Kofun Groups). Maebashi: Maebashi City Board of Education, June 2013, dd. 10–11. Excavations in 1968 revealed a clay coffin (internal length: 7.8 m, width: 1.4 m) at the center of the rear circular mound, containing 165 grave goods of 15 types. Prior excavations are believed to have occurred, but details remain unclear. In 1970, it was Historic site a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site}} by Gunma Prefecture. Presently, only the clay coffin remains preserved, as much of the mound was removed due to land development. File:Maebashi-tenjinnyama-kofun funchou.JPG|Clay coffin section at the mound top == Excavation History == In summer 1968, as the Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun faced imminent leveling, excavation began. By then, the front section had already been removed, and plans to level the rear section were underway after the investigation. While burial facilities were not identified during the survey, the excavation continued until a participating high school student discovered clay fragments on a cut surface, halting construction. The newly found burial facility was investigated in winter of the following year. Following the survey, the site's importance was recognized, and the remaining portions were preserved. == burial goods == [[File:前橋天神山古墳 出土品-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|excavations
(National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} (other images similarly).}}]] * Bronze mirrors: 5 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with four gods and four beasts: 1 ** Triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts: 1 ** Three-tiered mythical mirror: 1 ** Two birds and two beasts mirror: 1 ** Detypeed animal-shaped mirror: 1 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 * Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 * Iron swords: 6 * Iron blades: 12 * Small knives: 1 * Chisels: 23 * Gouges: 3 * Iron axes: 3 * Hook-like fittings: 6 * Needles: 6 * [[:en:quivers|quivers]]: 2 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Jar-shaped earthenware (containing red pigment): 1 The triangular-rimmed mirror with five gods and four beasts is from the same mold as mirrors found at {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} in Sakurai City and {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture.[http://www.emuseum.jp/detail/100615/000/000?mode=detail&d_lang=ja&s_lang=ja&class=10&title=&c_e=®ion=&era=¢ury=&cptype=&owner=&pos=49&num=2 excavations of Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun] - eNational Treasures These burial goods reflect the characteristics of early eastern Japanese kofun and have been Historic site a National Important designation1. They are housed at the Tokyo National Museum. Designated on June 6, 1979.{{designation1 Database|201|9982|burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important designation1: burial goods from Ueno Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} * Bronze mirrors ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors: 2 ** Mythical mirrors: 1 ** Bird and beast mirrors: 1 ** Woven design mirrors: 1 * Jade spindle whorls: 4 * Blades ** Sword with simple ring pommel: 1 ** Blades (including fragments): 6 ** Swords (including fragments): 12 * Bronze arrowheads: 30 * Iron arrowheads: 74 (including fragments) * Tools ** Iron axes (including fragments): 3 ** Iron chisels (including fragments): 23 ** Iron gouges: 3 ** Small knives: 1 * Iron hooks: 6 * Other excavated burial goods {{hidden end}} === Gunma Prefecture Historic site === * Historic sites ** Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun -
